Identity of other RFP participants beyond Coupa
items.quote: We are not just only considering Zycus. We are also including some other competitor in the market that is... equivalent level of you.
Our global CFO is... supervising us in his very few minutes that he has in his agenda.

metrics.narrative: Julio Lozano Cruz asked Zycus on 3 Dec 2025 for preliminary cost estimates comparing the full S2P suite against a Supplier Management (onboarding) plus eSourcing option, and stated the cheapest option would not automatically be preferred, with final criteria still pending internal team discussion. On 8 Apr 2026 Marc Quessenberry offered to provide a business case to help Antonino Cappuccio and Julio present to the relevant stakeholder. No quantified ROI, cost-avoidance, or cycle-time figures have been confirmed as delivered or accepted by the buyer in any source read.

champion.narrative: Julio Lozano Cruz is the most consistently engaged buyer contact: he drives scheduling, supplies information, and voiced favorable reactions ('it's pretty obvious that we select or invite you... we know that you are offering a good solution for us,' call, 3 Dec 2025). But he also told Zycus directly that Foundever is evaluating 'some other competitor in the market that is... equivalent level of you' (call, 13 Nov 2025), and there is no evidence of him advocating for Zycus with the CFO or Executive Committee, nor of him holding budget authority himself.

competition.narrative: Coupa is the named incumbent for transactional P2P and current e-sourcing; Foundever is negotiating a two-year Coupa ramp-down as SAP S/4HANA absorbs the transactional workload, but Coupa is resisting: per Julio, Coupa 'is not happy with the rundown, and at the same time, really interested in offering only the modules that I mentioned,' insisting on a bundle rather than the reduced scope Foundever wants (call, 5 Mar 2026). Foundever also confirmed on 13 Nov 2025 it is evaluating other RFP participants beyond Zycus and Coupa, though none were named in any source read.

identify_pain.narrative: Pain is extensively documented: supplier onboarding is described as lengthy today, e-sourcing runs through Coupa and requires an IT ticket to add an evaluator, and dual approval workflows across SAP ByDesign and Oracle only recently got consolidated (call, 5 Nov 2025). Foundever's wider driver is a 42-country Finance Transformation onto SAP S/4HANA Public Cloud (first go-live Jan 2027, full rollout by 2028), which absorbs transactional P2P but leaves CLM, sourcing, vendor onboarding and vendor management uncovered, exactly the gap Zycus is bidding to fill.

paper_process.narrative: No contracting artifacts (MSA, Order Form, SOW, redlines) have surfaced in any source; the deal is still in Formal Evaluation, ahead of shortlisting or vendor selection. The only paper-adjacent item is a mutual NDA Julio said he had forgotten to send and needed to send to the Zycus team to continue discussions (call, 13 Nov 2025); no confirmation of execution is in evidence.

economic_buyer.narrative: Foundever has had no global head of procurement since Miguel Gutierrez's departure around April 2025; in that gap a four-person procurement leadership group (Julio Lozano Cruz for the IT category, Andreina Woodworth for the PBS category, plus two regional procurement heads including Mariana Rodriguez, who owns the transformation) runs the evaluation collectively. Julio told Zycus directly on 13 Nov 2025 that Foundever's global CFO 'is supervising us in his very few minutes that he has in his agenda,' meaning budget approval sits with the Executive Committee but that person has never had direct contact with Zycus.

decision_process.narrative: The same four-person procurement committee runs the evaluation, with Julio as main point of contact ('I'm only the one for engaging with the candidates and taking a look at the commercials mostly,' call, 3 Dec 2025); a recommendation is meant to go to the Executive Committee for budget sign-off. Foundever's own stated target was a shortlist/award decision no later than end of Q2 2026 (30 Jun); as of the last update (2 Jun 2026) Julio said the committee was still reviewing submissions for down-selection, and that Q2 target is now roughly 9 days overdue as of today with no confirmed shortlist.

decision_criteria.narrative: Foundever issued a formal RFP on 18 Mar 2026 covering CLM, Sourcing, Vendor Management and Onboarding, due 31 Mar 2026, which Zycus met. A hard integration criterion surfaced on 5 Mar 2026: any onboarding/contracting module must validate vendor master data bi-directionally against the new SAP S/4HANA Public Cloud instance. Cost-optimization is an explicit named criterion (call, 3 Dec 2025), and the 5 and 13 Nov 2025 demos were driven by specific non-US, multi-currency, multi-language requirements.

CFO oversight is minimal. This creates decision drift and risk that no single person owns driving the deal to closure.

ESG reporting / spend analytics (iAnalyze) scope was flagged during a prior sales demo as needing must-have vs. nice-to-have clarification. If Foundever later decides iAnalyze is must-have and Zycus's iAnalyze capability does not meet their ESG reporting requirements, it could block the deal or force a scope/pricing revision.

Coupa is resisting Foundever's proposed 2-year P2P ramp-down and wants full license fees absent an agreement. Foundever called paying full Coupa fees a 'no-go'. If Coupa prevails, the budget available for the deal may shrink or Foundever may defer the decision until the Coupa contract expires.

No exec sponsor exists. If the procurement committee recommends Zycus but the CFO has no relationship with Zycus and no buy-in on the business case, the deal can be killed at final approval or sent back for re-scoping/re-pricing.

Foundever selected SAP S/4HANA Public Cloud and will use SAP for transactional P2P, with Zycus handling upstream CLM/eSourcing/Supplier Mgmt. The bi-directional integration (master data sync, contract-to-PO hand-off, invoice routing) is critical for Jan 2027 go-live but has not been scoped in a technical design doc or responsibility matrix. If integration complexity or SAP's implementation partner objects to the Zycus architecture, the deal is delayed or Foundever selects a simpler/SAP-native alternative.

critical_signals.text: Coupa is resisting Foundever's proposed two-year wind-down and is pushing to keep its modules as an all-or-nothing bundle rather than the narrower scope Foundever wants, which could squeeze the budget set aside for a new partner.

The most recent substantive update, about five weeks ago, was Julio confirming the committee is still reviewing submitted proposals for down-selection to a shortlist, with no decision date given, even though Foundever's own internal target for a decision (end of June) has now passed.

A hard integration requirement surfaced in March: any onboarding or contracting module must validate vendor master data both ways against the new SAP S/4HANA system, and this has not yet been scoped in a technical design document.

Foundever has been explicit that its decision will be cost-optimized rather than automatically going to the cheapest bid, and it is still waiting on the business case Zycus offered in April to support an Executive Committee funding request.

champion_strength.summary: Julio Lozano Cruz is warm, central, and process-driving but not a confirmed internal advocate at the level that controls the budget.

expansion_context.note: No sibling Closed-Won or related opportunity identified in the sources read.

north_star_verdict.math: 44 days to close (21 Aug 2026) vs. today 8 Jul 2026. RFP for CLM/Sourcing/Vendor Management/Onboarding was submitted on time (31 Mar 2026) and entered committee review; the last confirmed status (Julio Lozano, 2 Jun 2026, ~36 days ago) was still 'reviewing submissions for down-selection to shortlist.' Foundever's own internal target of a decision 'no later than end of Q2 2026' (30 Jun 2026) has now passed by 8 days with no confirmed shortlist. Against the buyer's stated Jan 2027 go-live, a compressed ~5-6 week window remains between shortlist, award, contracting and implementation kickoff if the 21 Aug close date is to hold.

north_star_verdict.headline: RFP submitted and under review, but the down-select decision has already blown past Foundever's own end-of-Q2-2026 target, and the CFO who must fund it has never been engaged.
north_star_verdict.recommended_forecast: Pipeline (no confirmed shortlist/finalist status, Economic Buyer never engaged, and the buyer's own decision deadline has already slipped)

Use Coupa's own resistance to the ramp-down and its end-of-2026 renewal as a forcing clock to press Foundever for a firm down-select date, since Foundever's internal end-of-Q2-2026 target has already passed.
Multi-thread beyond Julio to Antonino, Andreina, and Mariana (the named procurement transformation lead) directly, since Julio himself described the decision as collective with no single owner.

No champion: zero contact roles tagged Champion; no evidence of advocacy, internal selling, or EB introduction from any committee member. Engagement is professional but passive (SF Next_Step__c 2 Jun 2026: 'still reviewing').

Single-threaded at practitioner level: Julio is the primary contact (12 Next Step mentions), Antonino secondary; no multi-threading into finance (David Avila), legal, InfoSec, or C-suite despite 9-month cycle

Overdue milestone: shortlist decision expected 'week of 4/20' then 'no later than end of Q2 2026', now 5+ weeks late with no confirmed date (SF Next_Step__c 2 Jun 'still reviewing submissions'); compresses award/contract/implementation runway to Jan 2027 go-live

Unresolved funding gate: Coupa ramp-down conflict (Coupa demanding full license fees, Foundever calling it 'no-go') has not been resolved, creating budget-availability uncertainty (SF Next_Step__c 5 Mar 2026, no update since)

AI differentiator under-leveraged: Merlin, Merlin Intake, Agentic AI are in Products__c (SF authoritative), but no Next Step entry shows Zycus positioned Merlin as the strategic wedge vs. Coupa in RFP or follow-up conversations
Reinforce the ROI/differentiation story now to counter the flagged 'extend current Coupa' inertia risk -- quantify savings against the $100M indirect-spend baseline Julio already provided.

No Economic Buyer or CFO engagement: Executive Committee/CFO approval is required per Next_Step__c but no C-level contact exists in Salesforce or Avoma. Deal will stall or be rejected at final approval gate without EB relationship.

Jan 2027 go-live requires shortlist/award/contracting in next 6 weeks; further delay makes go-live unachievable and deal may be deferred or lost to faster alternative (timeline risk, forecast-critical)

Single-threaded through Julio Lozano (Global Category Manager IT); zero engagement with Antonino Cappuccio, Andreina Woodworth, or David Avila in 90+ days, and zero engagement ever with the CFO or Executive Committee who control final budget approval.
No champion identified or developed; Julio is a cooperative evaluator, not an advocate.

Shortlist decision 5+ weeks overdue: Foundever stated shortlist would occur 'no later than end of second quarter 2026' (30 Jun). As of 8 Jul the decision is overdue with no new timeline, no explanation, and no escalation by the buyer or deal owner's manager.
No competitive intelligence gathered since RFP submission 31 Mar; competitor names (beyond Coupa), RFP scoring, or Foundever's evaluation criteria remain unknown 99 days post-submission.

No SAP S/4HANA integration scoped in a technical design or responsibility matrix despite integration being critical path to Jan 2027 go-live; integration complexity or SAP partner objections could surface late as a deal-blocker.

Zero buyer calls in 2026: No Avoma calls discovered with Foundever attendees in 2026. All engagement is asynchronous via email/Salesforce updates, preventing real-time objection handling, competitive intelligence, or champion building.
Single-threaded: Julio Lozano is the sole active contact. Antonino Cappuccio (RFP lead) has not been engaged since April 2026. No executive, CFO, or multi-departmental relationships exist.

No quantified business case: Marc offered to provide a business case (8 Apr Next_Step__c) but there is no record of delivery. Executive Committee needs ROI/cost-avoidance to approve budget; without it, Foundever may default to lowest-cost vendor or defer decision.

No competitive positioning: 3-5 unnamed vendors submitted RFP responses. No competitive review, win-theme workshop, or trap-setting has occurred. Differentiation (Merlin, Agentic AI, SAP integration) exists on paper but has not been activated in buyer conversations.
No champion identified; primary contacts Julio and Antonino are neutral Evaluators conducting an RFP review, not advocates.

Single-threaded through committee: all 5 contact roles are Evaluators (Julio, Antonino, Andreina, David, Sadoc); no Economic Buyer, Decision Maker, or Executive Sponsor engaged. CFO oversight described as minimal (SF Next_Step__c 5 Mar 2026). Committee structure diffuses accountability and slows decision velocity.

Competitive intelligence gap: unknown who else is in RFP (likely SAP Ariba, GEP, Ivalua, Jaggaer inferred but not confirmed). No competitive review run in 90+ days. Cannot tailor wedge without knowing competitors.
Equip Julio Lozano with a competitive differentiation one-pager against the still-unnamed RFP rivals so he can actively advocate rather than remain a neutral process administrator.
Proactively surface that Foundever's own end-of-Q2-2026 decision target has lapsed and offer a joint mutual-close plan to protect the compressed runway to the stated Jan 2027 go-live.

Differentiate against Coupa's bundle-only stance by reinforcing Zycus's modular, unbundled scope (sourcing + onboarding + contracting only), since the buyer explicitly wants a reduced, cost-optimized footprint, not a full suite.
best_practice_check.summary: Sharpen the modular-vs-bundle differentiation against Coupa, broaden contact beyond Julio, and secure the first-ever executive-level touch before the shortlist decision.

customer_preference.evidence: Positive, specific buyer reactions ('very thorough and very interesting,' Andreina Woodworth, call 13 Nov 2025; 'it's pretty obvious that we select or invite you,' Julio Lozano Cruz, call 3 Dec 2025) are tempered by Julio's explicit confirmation that Foundever is evaluating other RFP participants at an equivalent level (call, 13 Nov 2025) and that cost-optimization, not preference, will drive the final call.

competitive_position.summary: Coupa is the incumbent being actively displaced from P2P transactional scope (moving to SAP S/4HANA) but is resisting Foundever's proposed 2-year ramp-down and pushing for full license-fee retention or a bundled deal (5 Mar 2026) -- the single most concrete current competitive dynamic, since an unfavorable outcome could push Foundever toward an interim vendor rather than expanding Zycus's footprint. A second, unnamed set of RFP rivals ('some other competitor... in equivalent level,' Julio Lozano, 13 Nov 2025) remains unidentified as of the last update; their standing in the down-select is unknown.

deal_scores_evidence.summary: The deal shows genuine, sustained buyer engagement (demos, an RFP round, a two-way email reply on 1 Jun 2026) but the actual decision, down-selection to a shortlist, has stalled past Foundever's own internal target, and the economic buyer has never engaged directly.

deal_risk.text: Coupa is resisting the proposed ramp-down and could force Foundever into a costlier fallback, squeezing the budget available for Zycus.

Julio Lozano Cruz is the primary day-to-day contact; Antonino Cappuccio, Andreina Woodworth and David Avila have all engaged in demos but have not been touched recently, leaving the relationship effectively single-threaded.

win_position.text: Foundever ran a full RFP round (issued 18 Mar 2026, response submitted 31 Mar 2026) and has repeatedly signaled favorable impressions of the Zycus demos through Julio Lozano Cruz and Andreina Woodworth.
No shortlist decision has been confirmed as of the last update (2 Jun 2026), and Foundever's own internal target for a decision (end of Q2 2026) has now passed.
The Foundever CFO/Executive Committee, which controls budget approval, has never had direct contact with Zycus (call, 13 Nov 2025).

deal_momentum.text: A genuine two-way buyer touch (incoming email reply) occurred on 1 Jun 2026, and further Salesforce activity was logged 23 Jun 2026, keeping the deal live rather than dark.

The Next Step has not advanced since 2 Jun 2026, and the gap between the promised week-of-20-Apr update and the eventual 2 Jun status shows the review process is running well behind Foundever's own stated pace.

Foundever confirmed on 13 Nov 2025 it is evaluating unnamed additional RFP participants at 'equivalent level' to Zycus.

hard.ais_why:
• The November 13 2025 demo (63 minutes) with Julio Lozano and six Foundever stakeholders is the strongest piece of evidence. Julio's reaction is captured as "Very, very pleasant surprise" at the feature set, and Andreina called the demo "very thorough and very interesting". The buyer engaged on AI-driven negotiation parameters, asking transparency questions on AI discount recommendations.
• A captured pain point reads "Need transparency on AI-driven discount recommendations to avoid overpaying". That is the buyer actively evaluating AI behaviour, not just being pitched. Products include Agentic AI, Merlin and Merlin Intake; AI importance is 'Important'; and the RFP for CLM, Sourcing and Vendor Management was responded to on 3/31.
• Multiple Foundever attendees - Antonino, Julio, Mariana, Andreina, David, Sadoc and Ana - joined the AI demo and asked detailed AI-related questions on multi-parameter AI negotiation, smart onboarding agent preview, and autonomous negotiation with market benchmarks. Strong cross-functional buyer engagement on AI-specific capabilities underpins the Hungry call.

Shortlist decision is 5+ weeks overdue (expected week of 4/20, then by end of Q2 2026, now 8 Jul 2026). Close date is 21 Aug 2026 (44 days out). Jan 2027 go-live for first 2 countries requires shortlist by mid-July, award by mid-Aug, contracting by early Sept, and implementation kickoff in Sept/Oct 2026. Current timeline is unachievable without severe compression or a slip in the Jan 2027 go-live, which may prompt Foundever to defer the decision or select a faster-to-deploy vendor.

The CFO or Executive Committee member who controls budget approval has never been engaged by the deal team. Next Step 3/18/26 states 'presenting a Business Case to the relevant stakeholder' and 'Once approval obtained, refining proposals during April to June for awarding one or two Partners', but no CFO name, contact, or engagement exists. Prior sweep notes describe 'CFO oversight as minimal'. If the procurement committee recommends Zycus but the CFO has no relationship with the deal team and no buy-in on the business case, the deal can be killed at final approval or sent back for re-scoping/re-pricing.

Foundever proposed a 2-year Coupa ramp-down, but 'Coupa is pushing back, renewal EOY' (Next Step 3/5/26). If Coupa demands full license fees absent an agreement, Foundever may lack budget for Zycus or be forced to select a cheaper interim vendor. The outcome of the Coupa negotiation is unknown and creates budget uncertainty for this deal.

No quantified business case (ROI, cost avoidance, cycle-time improvement) has been delivered despite Marc offering to provide one on 8 Apr 2026 and Foundever requiring it for Executive Committee approval (Next Step 3/18/26). value.name: Coupa
value.quote: Coupa is not happy with the rundown, and at the same time, really interested in offering only the modules that I mentioned... for them, for this company, it's just the bundle.
value.how_we_win: Position Zycus's modular, unbundled scope (sourcing, onboarding, vendor management, contracting only) directly against Coupa's bundle-only stance, since Foundever has explicitly said it does not want to pay for the full suite it no longer needs.

value.name: Coupa
value.quote: Coupa is resisting the proposed 2-year P2P ramp-down and demanding full license fees, which Foundever called a 'no-go.'
value.how_we_win: Coupa's resistance creates budget tension and opens the door for Zycus to absorb Coupa's transactional P2P scope if negotiations fail, expanding the deal. Position Zycus as the flexible, partnership-oriented alternative to Coupa's hardline renewal stance.

value.name: Coupa
value.quote: Foundever proposed to Coupa a 2yr. ramp down (Coupa is pushing back, renewal EOY). Looking to Zycus for CLM, eSourcing and Supplier Management with potential for additional footprint after Coupa ramp down. Want us to potentially replace existing Coupa P2P for 2 years if Coupa negotiations go south.
value.how_we_win: Position Zycus as the strategic, SAP-native CLM/eSourcing/Supplier Mgmt partner that can absorb Coupa's P2P scope if needed, with Merlin AI differentiation for negotiation intelligence and supplier onboarding automation, tied to Jan 2027 go-live certainty and phased global rollout support.

value.name: Coupa
value.quote: Coupa is pushing back, renewal EOY. They will be using SAP for transactional P2P and have proposed to Coupa a 2yr. ramp down. Want us to potentially replace existing Coupa P2P for 2 years if Coupa negotiations go south.
value.how_we_win: Leverage Foundever's frustration with Coupa's inflexibility; position Zycus as the cooperative partner willing to align commercial terms with the 2-year SAP transition, with Merlin Intake and Agentic AI as future-proof differentiation and proven SAP S/4HANA integration as the technical wedge.

value.name: Coupa

value.name: Coupa
value.quote: Currently using Coupa P2P and CLM. Unhappy with Coupa CLM and supplier onboarding.
value.how_we_win: Lead with the full integrated S2P suite (vs. Coupa's narrower onboarding/CLM footprint), Merlin's Teams-based conversational intake, and Gartner customer-choice recognition -- reinforce this now to counter the 'just extend Coupa' inertia risk flagged internally.
